title: Acridocarpus alternifolius
taxonomy: Acridocarpus alternifolius is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. It belongs to the genus Acridocarpus and is native to tropical Africa. The family Malpighiaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the Barbados cherry family. This family is part of the larger order Malpighiales, which includes many other flowering plant families. intro: Acridocarpus alternifolius, commonly known as the African cherry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Passifloraceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.
